title: Problem with stopping mysql 8
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create compose.yaml
 ```yaml
name: my_service

services:

 db:
    image: mysql:8.3
    environment:
      MYSQL_DATABASE: facturation_manager
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${DOCKER_DB_PASSWORD}
    # labels:
    #   - coolify.type=database
    #   - coolify.service.subType=database
    volumes:
      - db_data:/var/lib/mysql
    ports:
      - "${COOLIFY_DB_PORT:-3309}:3306"

volumes:
  db_data:
      
```

After I run the compose, when I try to stop the compose every time I get a different error.
```console
(base) sglbl@ddspc:~/deduce/vatsa$ type docker
docker is aliased to `podman'

(base) sglbl@ddspc:~/deduce/vatsa$ which docker-compose 
/usr/local/bin/docker-compose

(base) sglbl@ddspc:~/deduce/vatsa$ systemctl status podman.socket
● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/podman.socket; enabled; preset: enabled)
     Active: active (listening) since Wed 2026-03-25 15:26:13 CET; 19min ago
   Triggers: ● podman.service
       Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
     Listen: /run/podman/podman.sock (Stream)
     CGroup: /system.slice/podman.socket

mar 25 15:26:13 ddspc systemd[1]: Listening on podman.socket - Podman API Socket.
(base) sglbl@ddspc:~/deduce/vatsa$ 
```

```bash
Error response from daemon: container c4b5420b85bed31ad44bf40b1d26f1c619f2336559865dfaa6c12c36fff1cb28 
must be in Created or Stopped state to be started: container state improper

(base) sglbl@ddspc:~/deduce/vatsa$ podman container rm -fa
WARN[0030] StopSignal SIGTERM failed to stop container vatsa-db-1 in 30 seconds, resorting to SIGKILL 
5bf2ad25fb5cc4c8174674011ee9ffbf5d605cd9cc8ca59eb53d6df1157837f4
Error: cannot remove container c4b5420b85bed31ad44bf40b1d26f1c619f2336559865dfaa6c12c36fff1cb28 
as it could not be stopped: given PID did not die within timeout
```

I added stop_grace_period: 30s but didn't change anything. 

### Describe the results you received

Only way is to restart. It happened more than once.

### Describe the results you expected

To be able to stop mysql container

### podman info output


comments.body:
I join this issue, as I also experience it, with qbittorent-nox in my case.
I've created issue there https://github.com/qbittorrent/docker-qbittorrent-nox/issues/94 but one of the causes is podman ignoring "stop_grace_period" from container image.
From the image, or from the Compose YAML? Don't think images can set a stop timeout

I'm not sure how to answer ... so this is not an expert answer, more of what I know.
I did not user podman-compose.  I created the container initially with:
`podman run -d --name=qbit2 -it -v qbtconfig:/config -v "/mnt/d/qbt/qb:/downloads:rprivate,rbind" -v "/mnt/d/qbt/qbconf:/config:rprivate,rbind" -e QBT_WEBUI_PORT=8123 -e QBT_TORRENTING_PORT=12345 -e WEBUI_PORT=8080 -e QBT_USERNAME=toor -e QBT_PASSWORD=roottoor -p 8080:8080 -p 12345:12345 -p 12345:12345/udp -p 61684:61684 --dns=1.1.1.1 qbittorrentofficial/qbittorrent-nox:latest`
and someone recently hinted to also add "--stop-timeout 1800".

As the maintainer of qbittorrent-nox answered, their [stack yaml](https://github.com/qbittorrent/docker-qbittorrent-nox/blob/6cab9f5853fa06ae16d3cf5c4090d2a6dbe0f825/docker-stack.yml#L28) and [compose yaml](https://github.com/qbittorrent/docker-qbittorrent-nox/blob/6cab9f5853fa06ae16d3cf5c4090d2a6dbe0f825/docker-compose.yml) contains 
`stop_grace_period: 30m`
and from what I understand, other container software (docker desktop, rancher desktop) would respect such entry.

There also seems to be an issue with how Podman mounts Windows shares (as 9p volume). In my case this drive is quite fast nvme ssd, so it is not like "too slow hardware".  The workarount was moving config from windows mount to Podman Volume. But this is just going around the issue.

So, compose YAML, then. That is exactly identical to the `--stop-timeout` option you're using - so if that didn't work, adding an equivalent option to a Compose YAML won't work. I strongly suspect we support that in the Docker API as of now.

> I join this issue, as I also experience it, with qbittorent-nox in my case. I've created issue there [qbittorrent/docker-qbittorrent-nox#94](https://github.com/qbittorrent/docker-qbittorrent-nox/issues/94) but one of the causes is podman ignoring "stop_grace_period" from container image.

In my case, I also tried to get help from GenAIs (claude opus 4.6, gemini 3.1, chatgpt..) and even they couldn't solve it. One time opus was able to solve the issue by accessing inside the container change ownership etc. But rest of the times all they can say is "it's stuck just restart your pc".
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Has any one of you tested this?
Even though I use `podman pod rm -f ..` I get always get these errors. I never had these issues with docker and with podman everyday another bug.
